paper_title: Paleobathymetric Analysis of Elasmobranch Fauna from Montañita-Olón

methods.description: Collection and measurement of fossil elasmobranch specimens from the Montañita-Olón site for abundance and paleobathymetric analysis.

steps.description: All specimens were collected in situ from different points along the Montañita-Olón cliff section during several field trips conducted by the authors.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fossil specimens from Montañita-Olón site

steps.description: Photographs were taken with a Leica MZ16F multifocal stereomicroscope and Scanning Electronic Microscope (SEM) for small teeth.
steps.searchable_summary: capture images of specimens using microscopes

steps.description: Tooth measurements including total height, width and length were taken for the specimens and are listed in Table S1.

methods.description: Analysis of abundance for the Montañita-Olón site assemblage using percentages of specimens by family, genera, and species.

steps.description: An analysis of abundance for the Montañita-Olón site assemblage was carried out using percentages of specimens by family, genera and species.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate abundance percentages by taxonomic group

methods.description: Paleobathymetric analysis using two methods to estimate the depth distribution of the elasmobranch fauna from the Montañita-Olón site.

steps.description: A paleobathymetric analysis following the methodology of Nolf & Brzobohatý (1994), adapted to fossil sharks.
steps.searchable_summary: perform paleobathymetric analysis using Nolf's method

steps.description: A second paleobathymetric analysis following the methodology of Perez et al. (2017) was performed using R.
steps.searchable_summary: perform weighted bootstrap paleobathymetric analysis using R

steps.description: The data were resampled 10,000 times and plotted as a histogram that provides the mean depth.
steps.searchable_summary: resample data and plot histogram for mean depth

steps.description: The 95% confidence interval was obtained using a percentile method.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate 95% confidence interval using percentile method
